prompt: A linear function $f$ satisfies $f(-3) = 11$ and $f(5) = -5$. Which equation correctly defines $f(x)$?
The equation $4(2x - 3) = 3(x + 1) + 5x$ is solved below. Identify the step that contains the first error.
choices: Step 1 — the distributive property was applied incorrectly
Step 2 — like terms on the right were combined incorrectly
Step 3 — the subtraction of $8x$ was applied incorrectly
There is no error; the conclusion is correct

prompt: If $3x - 7 \equiv 2 \pmod{5}$, what is the smallest non-negative integer value of $x$ that satisfies this congruence? (Note: $a \equiv b \pmod{5}$ means $a - b$ is divisible by $5$.)
A function $g$ is defined by $g(x) = |2x - 6| - 4$. For which value(s) of $x$ does $g(x) = 0$?
Which inequality has the solution set shown on the number line: a closed circle at $-2$, open circle at $4$, shaded between them?
The sum of three consecutive even integers is $78$. What is the largest of the three integers?
Line $\ell$ passes through $(-1, 4)$ and is perpendicular to the line $3x - y = 7$. What is the $y$-intercept of line $\ell$?
Select ALL expressions equivalent to $(3x^2 y^{-1})^2 \cdot (x^{-3} y^2)^3$.
A rideshare company charges a flat booking fee plus a per-mile rate. A 4-mile trip costs $\$8.60$ and an 11-mile trip costs $\$15.25$. What is the per-mile rate?

Two cars depart from the same point in opposite directions. Car A travels at $55$ mph and Car B travels at $70$ mph. After how many minutes will the cars be exactly $62.5$ miles apart? Enter your answer as a whole number.

A manufacturer models profit $P$ (in dollars) as a function of units produced $x$: $P(x) = 12x - (180 + 4x)$. The break-even point occurs when $P(x) = 0$. How many additional units beyond break-even must be produced to earn a profit of at least $\$200$?
The system below models a real-world scenario. Which statement correctly interprets the solution?
info_box: where $x$ = number of adult tickets and $y$ = number of child tickets sold.
choices: $40$ adult tickets and $80$ child tickets were sold
$80$ adult tickets and $40$ child tickets were sold
$60$ adult tickets and $60$ child tickets were sold
$70$ adult tickets and $50$ child tickets were sold

prompt: Which of the following systems of equations have NO solution? Select ALL that apply.
A tank drains at a constant rate. At $t = 0$, the tank holds $480$ gallons. At $t = 12$ minutes, it holds $336$ gallons. At what time (in minutes) will the tank reach exactly $60$ gallons?

A contractor charges $\$45$ per hour for labor plus $\$320$ in materials. A client's total budget is no more than $\$1{,}010$. Which inequality models the maximum number of labor hours $h$ the contractor can work, and what is that maximum?
A student's savings account balance $B$ (in dollars) after $w$ weeks is modeled by $B = 1{,}240 - 65w$. Which statement BEST interprets the slope in this context?
choices: The account begins with $\$65$ and increases by $\$1{,}240$ each week
The account loses $\$65$ in value every week
After $1{,}240$ weeks, the account will be empty
The account decreases by $\$1{,}240$ for every $\$65$ withdrawn

prompt: The system $6x - 9y = 15$ and $2x - 3y = k$ has infinitely many solutions. What must be the value of $k$?
A system of two linear equations is graphed and the lines intersect at exactly one point. Select ALL statements that MUST be true.
choices: The system has a unique solution
Neither line passes through the origin

prompt: The graph of a linear function has slope $m$. If the function value decreases by $9$ when $x$ increases by $4$, and $f(2) = 3$, what is the $y$-intercept $b$?
Solve the system: $\dfrac{2}{3}x + \dfrac{1}{4}y = 5$ and $\dfrac{1}{2}x - \dfrac{3}{4}y = -1$. What is the value of $x + y$?

A nutritionist creates a meal plan using two food types. Each serving of Food A provides $8\text{g}$ protein and $200$ calories. Each serving of Food B provides $5\text{g}$ protein and $150$ calories. The plan must deliver at least $40\text{g}$ protein and stay below $1{,}050$ calories. If the plan uses $x$ servings of Food A and $y$ servings of Food B, which system of inequalities models all constraints?
The table below shows selected values of a linear function $h$. What is $h(-15)$?

A store sells pens at $\$p$ each and notebooks at $\$n$ each. An order of $6$ pens and $4$ notebooks costs $\$18.80$. An order of $9$ pens and $2$ notebooks costs $\$17.20$. What is the cost, in dollars, of $1$ pen and $1$ notebook together?
The solution to the compound inequality $-3 < 2 - x \leq 7$ is graphed on a number line. Which description correctly represents the solution?
choices: Open circle at $-5$, closed circle at $5$; shaded between
Closed circle at $-5$, open circle at $5$; shaded between
Open circle at $-5$, closed circle at $5$; shaded outside
Closed circle at $5$, open circle at $-5$; shaded between

prompt: The function $f(x) = 3x - 2$ is transformed to $g(x) = 3(x - 4) - 2$. Which transformation maps $f$ to $g$?
A piecewise function is defined as shown. What is $f(-1) + f(1) + f(4)$?
A function $f$ satisfies: $f(0) = 3$, $f$ is decreasing on $(-\infty, 2)$, $f$ is increasing on $(2, \infty)$, and $f(2) = -1$. Select ALL statements that are necessarily true.
The function $f$ is defined by the table below. What is the average rate of change of $f$ from $x = 3$ to $x = 7$?
The graph of a linear function passes through the second and fourth quadrants but NOT through the third quadrant. Which combination of slope and $y$-intercept is possible?
Starting with $f(x) = x$, three transformations are applied in order. Which equation represents the resulting function $g$?
info_box: 1. Multiply the output by $-2$ (vertical stretch and reflection across $x$-axis)
3. Subtract $5$ from the output (vertical shift down)

prompt: Four functions are graphed. Graph A has slope $2$ and $y$-intercept $-3$. Graph B passes through $(0, 4)$ and $(2, 0)$. Graph C is horizontal at $y = 1$. Graph D is a vertical line. Which graph represents a function with a rate of change of $-2$?
Select ALL representations that define $y$ as a function of $x$.
A company's revenue $R(t)$ and cost $C(t)$ (both in thousands of dollars) over $t$ months are given. At what month $t$ does revenue first exceed cost?
choices: $t = 10$; the company first becomes profitable after $10$ months
$t = 11$; revenue first exceeds cost during month $11$
$t = 30$; revenue will always exceed cost after $30$ months
$t = 6$; the costs and revenues are equal at month $6$

prompt: A function $f$ is defined by $f(x) = \dfrac{3x - 6}{x^2 - 4}$. For how many integer values of $x$ in the interval $[-5,\; 5]$ is $f(x)$ undefined?
Three representations of functions are given. Which statement correctly compares their rates of change?
choices: $P$ has the greatest rate of change
$Q$ has the greatest rate of change
$R$ has the greatest rate of change
$Q$ and $R$ have equal rates of change

prompt: Simplify completely: $(4x^3 - 9) \div (2x - 3)$. Assume $2x - 3 \neq 0$.
The product $(2x + 5)(3x - 4) - (x - 3)^2$ is written in the form $ax^2 + bx + c$. What is the value of $a + b + c$?
The area of a rectangle is represented by $6x^2 + 11x - 10$. If the width of the rectangle is $(2x + 5)$, what is the length?
Select ALL expressions equal to $\dfrac{(2^3)^4}{4^2 \cdot 2^{-1}}$.
The expression $x^2 - 14x + 49 - 4y^2$ can be written as a product of two binomial factors. What are those factors?
The quadratic $f(x) = -2x^2 + 12x - 7$ has a maximum value. What is that maximum value?
For which values of $x$ is $x^2 - x - 12 < 0$?
A quadratic function has zeros at $x = -1$ and $x = 5$, and its leading coefficient is $-3$. Which equation defines this function?
The equation $x^2 + bx + 36 = 0$ has exactly two real solutions. Select ALL integer values of $b$ for which this is true.
If $4^{x+1} = 8^{x-1}$, what is the value of $x$?
Find the value of $3x - 2y$, where $(x, y)$ is the solution to the system below.

A pump fills a pool at rate $r_1$ gallons/hour while a drain removes water at rate $r_2$ gallons/hour. After $3$ hours, $270$ net gallons have been added. After $7$ hours, $490$ net gallons have been added. What is the net fill rate $r_1 - r_2$ in gallons per hour?

A linear model $T(d)$ gives temperature $T$ (in °F) at depth $d$ (in feet) inside a mine shaft. $T(100) = 68$ and temperature increases by $1.5{\text{ °F}}$ for every additional $10$ feet of depth. Select ALL true statements.
choices: The rate of change is $0.15$ °F per foot

prompt: Two cell phone plans are modeled below. For what range of minutes is Plan A the better deal (lower cost)?
The parabola $f(x) = x^2 - 8x + c$ has a vertex whose $y$-coordinate equals $-7$. What is the value of $c$?
For what value(s) of $k$ does the equation $(k - 3)x = 2k - 6$ have infinitely many solutions?
A feasible region is defined by the system below. Select ALL ordered pairs that lie within the feasible region (including boundary).

A company sells two product models. In January: $3$ units of Model X and $7$ units of Model Y totaled $\$1{,}670$ in revenue. In February: $5$ units of Model X and $2$ units of Model Y totaled $\$1{,}180$. In March, the company plans to sell $4$ units of each model. What will March's total revenue be, in dollars?
A student solves $\dfrac{x^2 - 4}{x - 2} = x + 2$ and concludes $x$ = all real numbers. Which statement about this conclusion is correct?
choices: The student is correct; every real number satisfies the equation
The student is incorrect; $x = 2$ is an extraneous solution because it makes the denominator zero
The student is incorrect; only $x > 2$ satisfies the equation
The student is incorrect; both $x = 2$ and $x = -2$ are excluded

prompt: Line $\ell$ passes through $(-2, 1)$ and has slope $\dfrac{3}{2}$. Which labeled point is the $y$-intercept of line $\ell$?
info_box: Use the slope and the given point to determine where the line crosses the $y$-axis.
